title: Fermented Crisp Syrian Lamb Broth
description: A tangy, protein-rich lamb broth with a fresh, crisp vegetable topping, perfect for a postworkout recovery meal.

ingredients.name: lamb bones
ingredients.notes: preferably from a butcher for best flavor

instructions: In a large stockpot, combine lamb bones and cold water; bring to a gentle boil, skim off scum.
Add carrot, celery, onion, garlic, bay leaf, peppercorns, and salt; reduce heat and simmer for 3 hours.
After 3 hours, strain the stock through a fine sieve, discarding solids, and cool to 40°C (104°F).
Stir in fresh coriander, lemon zest, dill, and red pepper flakes; reduce the stock by half over medium heat until slightly thickened (about 20 minutes).
Transfer the reduced stock to a bowl and whisk in strained Greek yogurt until smooth, keeping the mixture chilled.
Gently reheat the yogurt‑infused broth to 70°C (158°F) without boiling to preserve the fermented flavor.
In a skillet, quickly sauté diced cucumber, tomato, parsley, and a pinch of salt until just wilted (about 2 minutes).
Serve a ladle of the warm broth in bowls; top with the crisp vegetable mixture.
Garnish with additional dill, a squeeze of fresh lemon, and a drizzle of extra‑virgin olive oil.
Enjoy the broth hot for a protein‑rich post‑workout recovery.
